Scenario.title: Ransomware Attack on Healthcare Provider via Phishing
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A ransomware attack targeting a healthcare provider leverages phishing emails containing malicious documents to gain initial access, exploits unpatched Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerabilities to execute payloads, and encrypts critical electronic health records to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, File System
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Application Log Content, Data Encrypted for Impact

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends a malicious email with an infected document attachment to healthcare staff, exploiting CVE-2021-40444 (Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerability) to compromise the victim's email system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker leverages unpatched Exchange Server vulnerabilities to execute arbitrary code via the downloaded payload, gaining control over the compromised system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware is deployed to encrypt electronic health records (EHRs) across the network, rendering critical healthcare operations inoperable and demanding a ransom payment.

Scenario.summary: A basic-level ransomware campaign compromises a healthcare provider through phishing, exploits unpatched Exchange Server software, and encrypts patient data to extort financial gain.
